causeCollection.description:
A lower-level set of structured errors.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

causeIndexedCollection.description:
A lower-level set of structured errors.
Each of these errors MUST have index set.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

causeIsInternal.description: If true, then the cause (`causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection`) should be hidden from the user.

causeSimple.description:
Simple string error type.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

domain.description:
The "domain" for the error.  If empty, it is an EDA
core error.  Alternatively it can be an EDA application
"apiVersion" value (e.g. interfaces.eda.nokia.com/v1alpha1)
indicating that the error is specific to that application.
The domain gives the receiver information that they can use
to help them interpret the "type" field.

index.description:
When processing an array, errors may be generated related to a particular array item.
If set, this index indicates to which array item the error applies.

message.description: The basic text error message for the error response.

ref.description: Reference to the error source. Should typically be the URI of the request.

type.description:
Type defines a unique identifier for the error, within the domain.
This may be used (along with the domain) to find an internationalization translation for the message.

SHOULD be a valid golang identifier, and SHOULD be in UpperCamelCase.

values.description:
Associated data/information.
The error "message" may contain {{name}} escapes that should be substituted with information from this dictionary.

Note that this map MUST NOT contain JSON objects (`{...}`) or arrays (`[...]`), only simple JSON types are permitted.

ErrorResponse.title: Generic error response for REST APIs.

K8SPatchOp.description: The definition for a JSON patch for a resource, as in RFC 6902

from.description:
Only applies to a "move" operation, and is the path to the field/value within
the target JSON object that should be moved to the location specified by the "path"
field.

op.description: The type of operation this patch should perform.

path.description:
The path to the field within the target JSON object
that should be patched according to the "op" field.

value.description:
This field would be set to the value which the user intends to put in the
field referenced by the "path" field.  The type of the value should match
the type of the field as indicated by the schema of the resource being
patched.

x-permissive.description:
For an "add" operation, an already-present field referenced by the "path" will
result in an error unless this field is true.  Similarly for a non-existent
target field of a "remove" operation.

Status.title: Status is a return value for calls that don't return other objects.

dot1pRewritePolicy.description: Dot1pRewritePolicy enables the configuration of rewrite policies for Dot1p values. It includes mappings of forwarding classes to Dot1p values, with options for drop probability-specific overrides within each forwarding class.

dot1pMap.description: Map of forwarding classes to PCP values

dropProbabilities.description: Drop probability specific overrides within the forwarding class

level.description: A drop probability level within the forwarding class for which a different remarking is desired
level.title: Level

pcpValue.description: The PCP value to be used for packets associated with the forwarding class and the specific drop probability. This overrides the general PCP value.
pcpValue.title: PCP Value

dropProbabilities.title: Drop Probability

forwardingClasses.description: The forwarding class matched to apply the rewrite policy
forwardingClasses.title: Forwarding Class

pcpValue.description: The PCP value to be used for all packets associated with the forwarding class, except those with a drop-probability-specific or profile-specific override
pcpValue.title: PCP Value

dot1pMap.title: Map

dot1pRewritePolicy.title: Dot1p Rewrite Policy

dscpRewritePolicy.description: DSCPRewritePolicy enables the configuration of rewrite policies for Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) values. It includes mappings of forwarding classes to DSCP values, with options for drop probability-specific overrides within each forwarding class.  If a DSCPRewritePolicy is not specified, the DSCP value of the packet is unchanged. If a DSCP policy is specific and ECN is enabled on any of the queues, the DSCP policy will be applied to all ECN capable packets.

dscpMap.description: Map of forwarding classes to DSCP values.

dropProbabilities.description: A drop probability within the forwarding class for which a different remarking is desired.

dscp.description: The DSCP value to be used for packets associated with the forwarding class and the specific drop probability. This overrides the general DSCP value.
dscp.title: DSCP

level.description: A drop probability level within the forwarding class for which a different remarking is desired.
level.title: Level

dropProbabilities.title: Drop Probability

dscp.description: The DSCP value to be used for all packets associated with the forwarding class, except those with a drop-probability-specific or profile-specific override.
dscp.title: DSCP

forwardingClasses.description: The forwarding class matched to apply the rewrite policy.
forwardingClasses.title: Forwarding Class

dscpMap.title: Map

dscpRewritePolicy.title: DSCP Rewrite Policy

forwardingClassToQueueMapping.description: Forwarding class to queue mapping policy.

forwardingClasses.description: The forwarding classes to which the mapping applies, these are references to ForwardingClass resources.
forwardingClasses.title: Forwarding Classes

queue.description: The queue to which the forwarding classes are mapped, this is a reference to a Queue resource.
queue.title: Queue

queueGroup.description: The queue-group name for queue to forwarding class mapping.
queueGroup.title: Queue Group

forwardingClassToQueueMapping.title: Forwarding Class to Queue Mapping

pfcDeadlockAvoidance.description: Parameters related to avoid a deadlock related to pfc on outgoing interface.

deadlockAvoidance.description: Parameters related to avoid a deadlock related to pfc on outgoing interface
deadlockAvoidance.title: Deadlock Avoidance

deadlockDetectionTimerMs.description: Number of milliseconds during which outgoing interface is receiving pfc-pause-frames before triggering recovery-timer.
deadlockDetectionTimerMs.title: Deadlock Detection Timer

deadlockRecoveryTimerMs.description: Number of milliseconds during which the pfc-pause-frames will be ignored.
deadlockRecoveryTimerMs.title: Deadlock Recovery Timer

pfcDeadlockAvoidance.title: Deadlock Avoidance

queueManagement.description: Queue management policy for egress queues.

maximumBurstSizeBytes.description: Maximum amount of shared buffer memory available to the queue in bytes.
maximumBurstSizeBytes.title: Maximum Burst Size

schedulerPeakRatePercent.description: The peak rate percent used by the scheduler for the queue.
schedulerPeakRatePercent.title: Scheduler Peak Rate Percent

schedulerPriorityLevel.description: The priority level at this Port Scheduler Policy.
schedulerPriorityLevel.title: Scheduler Priority Level

schedulerWeight.description: The weight factor used for the WRR scheduler. If any of the queues have a configured weight the set of queues will use a WRR scheduler and thus all queues must have a weight configured.  If no weights are set then the queues are scheduled in strict priority from lowest to higher queue ID.
schedulerWeight.title: Scheduler Weight

slopePolicyWeight.description: The average queue size is calculated using both the previous average and the current queue size: average = (previous average)(1 - 2^(-n)) + (current size)(2^(-n)), where n is a user-configurable weight factor. A higher n gives more importance to the previous average, smoothing peaks and lows in the queue. Lower n makes the average closer to the current queue size. If this leaf is absent, the default value is used.
slopePolicyWeight.title: Slope Policy Weight

wredSlopePolicies.description: Slope policy to apply to the set of queues.

dropProbability.description: The drop probability level to which the WRED slope applies.
dropProbability.title: Drop Probability

ecn.description: When set to true and the queue length is between the thresholds and the ECN field indicates ECN-capable endpoints, the CE bits are set to 1, and the packet is transmitted based on WRED. If false, such packets are discarded.
ecn.title: ECN

maxDropProbabilityPercent.description: If the queue depth is between min and max threshold then this the probability with which packets are dropped or marked.
maxDropProbabilityPercent.title: Max Drop Probability Percent

maxThresholdBytes.description: The maximum threshold parameter for a RED-managed queue in bytes. When the average queue length exceeds the max value, all packets are dropped (or marked if ECN is enabled). Mutually exclusive with min-threshold-percent and max-threshold-percent.
maxThresholdBytes.title: Max Threshold

maxThresholdPercent.description: The maximum threshold parameter for a RED-managed queue in percent. When the average queue length exceeds the max value, all packets are dropped (or marked if ECN is enabled). Mutually exclusive with min-threshold and max-threshold.
maxThresholdPercent.title: Max Threshold Percent

minThresholdBytes.description: The minimum threshold parameter for a RED-managed queue in bytes. When the average queue length is less than min, all packets are admitted to the queue. Mututally exclusive with min-threshold-percent and max-threshold-percent.
minThresholdBytes.title: Min Threshold

minThresholdPercent.description: The minimum threshold parameter for a RED-managed queue in percent. When the average queue length is less than min, all packets are admitted to the queue. Mutually exclusive with min-threshold and max-threshold.
minThresholdPercent.title: Min Threshold Percent

trafficType.description: The traffic type to which the WRED slope applies.
trafficType.title: Traffic Type

wredSlopePolicies.title: Slope Policy

queueManagement.title: Queue Management

classifier.description: Classifier manages the configuration of traffic classification policies in a network. It includes various entry types like IPv4, IPv6, Dot1p, and DSCP policies. Each entry specifies how traffic should be classified and what actions should be taken on the matched packets.

entries.description:
Specifies the list of filter entries, in order.
A classifier containing multiple entry types may result in multiple classifiers being created on the target node.
IPV4 and IPV6 entries will create multifield classifier policies.

dot1pPolicyEntry.description: A Dot1p policy entry - only a single Dot1p entry is allowed per classifier resource.

directToPFCQueue.description: In addition to creating a Dot1p PCP value to Forwarding Class mapping, this will map the PCP values directly to the PFC queue specified in the Forwarding Class to Queue mapping.
directToPFCQueue.title: Direct to PFC Queue

dropProbabilityLevel.description: Assign matching packets to the specified drop probability level.
dropProbabilityLevel.title: Drop Probability Level

forwardingClass.title: Forwarding Class

pcpValues.description: List of PCP values or ranges used to match packets to classify into Forwarding Classes.

rangeEnd.description: Optional end of PCP range (inclusive) which would start from the Value to the RangeEnd.
rangeEnd.title: PCP Range End

value.description: Single PCP value or start of range.
value.title: PCP Value

dot1pPolicyEntry.title: Dot1p Policy Entry

destinationPortNumber.description: Destination port to match by numerical value.

destinationPortRange.description: Range of destination ports to match, in the format n-m, e.g. 100-200,  The start and end of the range must be port numbers.
destinationPortRange.title: Destination Port Range

icmpCodes.description: Match a specific ICMP code, as a number between 0-255, e.g. 0.
icmpCodes.title: ICMP Code

icmpTypeName.description: Match a specific ICMP type by name, e.g. dest-unreachable.
icmpTypeName.title: ICMP Type Name

protocolName.description: Match a specific IP protocol name (specified in the type field of the IP header).
protocolName.title: Protocol Name

tcpFlags.description: Match TCP flags, usable with !, &, | and the flags RST, SYN, and ACK.

committedBurstSizeBytes.description: Maximum CIR bucket depth in bytes.
committedBurstSizeBytes.title: Committed Burst Size

committedRateKbps.description: The committed information rate (CIR) of the policer, defined in kilobits (1024 bits) per second.

committedRatePercent.description: The committed information rate (CIR) of the policer, defined as a percentage of the Interface speed on which it is applied.
committedRatePercent.title: Committed Rate Percentage

exceedAction.description: Applies a drop-probability to packets that the policer has determined are exceeding (yellow).

dropProbabilityLevel.description: The drop probability level to apply.
dropProbabilityLevel.title: Drop Probability Level

exceedAction.title: Exceed Action

forwardingClasses.description: The list of forwarding classes with traffic to be sent to the policer.  Unless specified all traffic is matched for this policer.
The forwarding class of the packets on which to apply the Policer.  To match all traffic set this to 'ALL'.
forwardingClasses.title: Forwarding Class

forwardingTypes.description: The traffic type to which the Policer applies.
forwardingTypes.title: Forwarding Type

forwardingClasses.title: Forwarding Class

maximumBurstSizeBytes.description: Maximum PIR bucket depth in bytes.
maximumBurstSizeBytes.title: Maximum Burst Size

minInterfaceSpeed.description: Minimum interface speed (kbps) to calculate PeakRate and CommittedRate for devices where configuration is not supported in percentage.
minInterfaceSpeed.title: Minimum Interface Speed

peakRateKbps.description: The peak information rate (PIR) of the policer, defined in kilobits (1024 bits) per second.
peakRateKbps.title: Peak Rate

peakRatePercent.description: The peak information rate (PIR) of the policer, defined as a percentage of the Interface speed on which it is applied.
peakRatePercent.title: Peak Rate Percentage

violateAction.description: Applies a drop-probability to packets that the policer has determined are exceeding (red).

dropProbabilityLevel.title: Drop Probability Level

violateAction.title: Violate Action

pfcReservedBufferPercent.description: Percentage of the linecard buffer reserved for accomodating incoming traffic while upstream node reacts to generated PFC-pause frames. Note: this percentage must be common across all EgressPolicies and QueuesSets used on the same linecard.
pfcReservedBufferPercent.title: PFC Reserved Linecard Buffer Percent

pfcPauseFramePriority.description: PFC priorities indicated in generated pfc-pause-frame if congestion occurs in a given pfc-queue.
pfcPauseFramePriority.title: PFC Priority
